title: There Was A Lady Loved A Swine
content:
1.
    There was a lady loved a swine,
    "Honey!" said she;
    "Pig-hog, wilt thou be mine?"
    "Hunc!" said he.

2.
    "I'll build thee a silver sty,
    Honey!" said she;
    "And in it thou shalt lie!"
    "Hunc!" said he.

3.
    "Pinned with a silver pin,
    Honey!" said she;
    "That thou mayest go out and in,"
    "Hunc!" said he.

4.
    "Will thou have me now,
    Honey?" said she;
    "Speak, or my heart will break,"
    "Hunc!" said he.
meaning:
"There Was A Lady Loved A Swine" is a quintessential example of Walter Crane's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
